{{Quote|This is a case in which we were able to exploit the Empire's nonacceptance of alien species. In treating aliens as lesser beings, many Imperials became incapable in seeing unusual alien life-forms as intelligent. Indeed, vuv Tertarrnek's work would be much more difficult—perhaps impossible—with more enlightened foes.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], on the Covallon spy Dælar vuv Tertarrnek|<ref name="CRO" />}}
The '''Covallon''' were a bestial [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] rarely seen away from their [[Unidentified Covallon homeworld|homeworld]]. The descendants of swift predatory quadrupeds, the Covallon had a developed capacity for sensing others' emotions, a holdover from [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] in packs. Covallon society developed to an early information technology level by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], although most Covallon were comfortable with more advanced technology. The Covallon [[Dælar vuv Tertarrnek]] served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as a [[Spy/Legends|spy]], having been given to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Harlov Jarnek]] as a pet. From that position vuv Tertarrnek fed the Alliance with strategic information on Imperial [[Imperial Army/Legends|troop]] and [[Imperial Navy/Legends|fleet]] movements in the [[Tandon sector/Legends|Tandon sector]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Covallon were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. They were quadrupeds, having evolved from a swift plains-based predator species. They preferred to walk on all four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], although they were physically capable of walking upright when the need arose. On all fours, they averaged a height of 0.9–1.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] at the shoulder; while standing on their hind legs, they averaged between 1.8–2.2 meters. Their lower limbs ended in three-toed feet, while their upper limbs ended in four-fingered hands; the digits on both hands and feet were tipped with a sharp claw. Their hands were dexterous and capable of fine manipulation. The Covallon also had a long tail.<ref name="CRO" />

The Covallon were largely bald, with a pattern of darker markings across their skin. However, a thick mane of hair grew around their shoulders and upper arms. The Covallon head was bald and had a central, backward-pointing horn. They had two small [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and a blunt muzzle with a jaw filled with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], a reflection of their carnivorous nature. A number of thin tendrils grew out of each side of their jaws. Overall, the Covallon had a bestial appearance—at least to [[Human/Legends|Human]] eyes.<ref name="CRO" />

==Society and culture==
Despite being an ancient species, Covallon society remained at an early information technology level; however, Covallon were also comfortable with more advanced [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] machines and technology. They took a long view of events, preferring to wait out adversity rather than take premature action. They invested a significant amount of time planning things before taking action. The Covallon ancestors [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] in packs. In modern Covallon, that manifested in a strong empathetic ability, enabling them to sense the emotions of others. As a consequence, Covallon worked very well in teams, functioning like a finely-crafted machine.<ref name="CRO" />

Covallon did not feel the need to wear clothing; however, they generally wore clothing of some sort out of protection or for practical purposes. It was common for clothed and unclothed Covallon to mingle freely.<ref name="CRO" /> The Covallon could understand [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">{{CSWECite|III|315|Dælar vuv Tertarrnek}}</ref> Covallon naming conventions included the use of "æ"<ref name="CRO" /> (the [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] letter [[Enth/Legends|Enth]])<ref name="Star Wars Gamemaster Screen, Revised">''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen, Revised]]''</ref> as well as two-part surnames in which the first particle was de-capitalized.<ref name="CRO" />

==History==
The Covallon were an ancient species that evolved from a quadruped predator. They developed a low-level information technology society at which point development largely plateaued. They were rarely seen away from their [[Unidentified Covallon homeworld|homeworld]], and as a consequence, few beings were familiar with their species. Those Covallon who did venture forth from their homeworld often encountered condescension from more [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] species, who viewed them more as creatures than intelligent beings.<ref name="CRO" />

==Covallon in the galaxy==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Covallon [[Dælar vuv Tertarrnek]] served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as a [[Spy/Legends|spy]]. The Alliance arranged for vuv Tertarrnek to be given to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Harlov Jarnek]], ruler of [[Tandon sector/Legends|Tandon sector]], as a pet. Jarnek was unaware of the intelligence of vuv Tertarrnek and, consequently, became very fond of his "pet." Vuv Tertarrnek accompanied Jarnek almost everywhere, including high-security conferences. From that position, vuv Terkarrnek obtained confidential information relating to Imperial [[Imperial Army/Legends|troop]] and [[Imperial Navy/Legends|fleet]] movements within and through the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], as well as top-priority codes. Vuv Tertarrnek then relayed that information to the Alliance in coded bursts from a long-range, tight-beam communication hidden within [[Moff Jarnek's palace]] on [[Spirador]].<ref name="CRO" /> By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Rebel Alliance was preparing a plan to arrest Jarken on Spirador, considering him a threat based on the large number of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] under his control.<ref name="ANH">''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Covallon were created by [[Stephen Crane]] for the [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', published in [[1994]]. It contained one illustration of a Covallon, drawn by [[Mike Vilardi]].<ref name="CRO" />
